rho: High-volume commercial devices and low-volume mission-critical radios have very different implementation thresholds.

envelope.interval_scope: research-input sensitivity with dataset anchors (l, n) held fixed

identity.title: Radio and Television Broadcasting and Wireless Communications Equipment Manufacturing

lens.customer_and_revenue_model: Repeat equipment, platform, accessory, license, support, and service sales to carriers, broadcasters, cable operators, enterprises, governments, public-safety agencies, defense customers, integrators, distributors, and consumers, priced by performance, frequency, capacity, configuration, qualification, volume, support, and procurement terms.
lens.excluded_activities: Captive plants, telecom and broadcasting services, household televisions and radios, test instruments, pure software vendors, distributors, installers without manufacturing, passive entities, and firms without transferable operations.
lens.included_activities: Independent U.S. firms repeatedly designing, assembling, manufacturing, testing, and selling wireless communications, antenna, GPS, mobile radio, cable television, and broadcast or studio transmission equipment to external customers.

a.bridge: BLS measures broader 334200 and reports 16.90% of employment in computer occupations and 14.22% in management, plus engineering, sales, office, production, maintenance, and logistics. NIST documents manufacturing AI uses in quality, maintenance, forecasting, scheduling, and resource management. I translate this occupational and use-case evidence into wage-weighted remaining exposure for wireless and broadcast equipment. Extensive electronic design and factory automation lower the not-yet-automated share.
caveats: OEWS combines 334210, 334220, and 334290.
Public-safety radios, consumer wireless products, carrier infrastructure, antennas, and broadcast systems have different labor mixes.
a.change_evidence: A 334220 task-and-wage study across radio, antenna, cellular, GPS, cable, and broadcast equipment makers separating existing automation from remaining work would materially change the range.
a.rationale: AI can assist RF and firmware engineering, simulation, test analysis, spectrum and interference diagnostics, configuration, documentation, visual inspection, forecasting, sourcing, proposals, support, and administration. Physical assembly, calibration, rework, certification, secure validation, field interoperability, and accountable release remain.

d5.bridge: BLS projects broader 334200 real output from $54.5 billion in 2024 to $66.5 billion in 2034, a 2.0% annual rate. Vistance Networks highlights Wi-Fi 7, 6 GHz, DOCSIS 4.0, 5G convergence, and network automation, while BK Technologies serves enduring public-safety and government radio needs. I convert the broader projection and segment drivers into a five-year constant-quality ratio; wired equipment in 334200 may overstate 334220 growth, while defense, private wireless, and satellite-related niches may outperform.
caveats: BLS 334200 includes wired and other communications equipment and measures real value, not physical units.
The industry's product categories have divergent cycles, and U.S. demand need not translate to U.S. manufacture.
d5.change_evidence: A five-year Dell'Oro, Omdia, ABI Research, or government procurement forecast of U.S. shipments by radio access, Wi-Fi, antenna, public-safety radio, broadcast, cable, GPS, and cellular-device category, reconciled to domestic production, would materially improve the estimate.
d5.rationale: 5G and private wireless, Wi-Fi upgrades, public safety, defense, satellite connectivity, spectrum modernization, cable upgrades, and video production support demand. Standards transitions, carrier capital cycles, consumer commoditization, software-defined functions, consolidation, imports, and offshore handset production create downside.

caveats: Establishments are not firms and the code is unusually heterogeneous.
The 25.32% Computers/Peripherals margin bridge may overstate EBITDA for contract-heavy or price-pressured equipment businesses.
e.change_evidence: A named review of the 210 candidates covering ultimate ownership, domestic manufacturing substance, external repeat revenue, normalized EBITDA, product category, customer concentration, certifications, export controls, IP, and transferability would directly measure eligibility.
e.rationale: Census reports 645 employer establishments and the frozen count identifies 210 firms in the EBITDA band, plausibly including numerous antenna, RF subsystem, public-safety radio, broadcast, GPS, wireless-network, and specialty-equipment makers. Large groups, captive and defense units, import-led brands, component-only suppliers, project-heavy firms, weak margins, and businesses outside the band reduce eligibility.

l.detail: QCEW 2024 private total annual wages at 334220 ($6,421,482,536) x benefits multiplier 1.4294 [ECEC private-industry total comp $46.60/hr / wages $32.60/hr = 1.4294 (March 2026 reference period)] / SUSB 2022 receipts at 334220 ($31,389,539,000). Vintage mismatch: 2024 wages over 2022 receipts, stated. Level: 6-digit. Harmonized to IPS basis ÷1.3407 (median of 65 dual-method codes).

n.detail: margin 0.2532 (margins.json[334]: Damodaran Jan 2026: Computers/Peripherals EBITDA/Sales 25.32%); revenue/employee $630,616 (SUSB 2022 receipts/employment at 334220); class <5: 188 firms, avg revenue/firm $837,245 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $211,990 -> out of $1-10M band; class 5-9: 103 firms, avg revenue/firm $3,389,359 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $858,186 -> out of $1-10M band; class 10-19: 74 firms, avg revenue/firm $4,224,162 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $1,069,558 -> IN $1-10M band; class 20-99: 136 firms, avg revenue/firm $16,564,331 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $4,194,089 -> IN $1-10M band; class 100-499: 46 firms, avg revenue/firm $61,394,630 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $15,545,120 -> out of $1-10M band; class 500+: 48 firms, avg revenue/firm $531,115,479 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $134,478,439 -> out of $1-10M band; n_band = 210

caveats: Imports can displace domestic operators without eliminating operator-required demand.
Software-defined networking shifts value and hardware architecture and is reflected partly in d5.
o.change_evidence: Evidence of rapid software or shared-infrastructure substitution eliminating specific equipment categories, customer self-manufacture, or autonomous contract production removing the lens operator would lower the estimate.
o.rationale: Remaining wireless and broadcast demand requires an accountable maker to design or integrate, test, calibrate, certify, secure, warrant, and deliver physical radios, antennas, transmitters, receivers, and related equipment. Software-defined functions can reduce dedicated hardware but cannot eliminate the physical radio-frequency layer.

q.bridge: Vistance Networks reports competition on specifications, quality, price, service, and delivery, while Airgain reports price-conscious customers and pressure that can force prices below list. BK Technologies shows differentiated public-safety radio products and government customers. I translate these vendor disclosures to the broad LMM lens: standardized commercial equipment passes gains through, while mission-critical qualification, proprietary RF design, installed-base compatibility, security, support, and small-volume customization improve retention.
caveats: The cited vendors represent different subsectors and are larger than many target firms.
Consumer wireless, broadcast, carrier, satellite-adjacent, and public-safety products have materially different pricing power.
q.change_evidence: Eligible-firm contracts, bid tabs, discount histories, cost and tariff pass-through, support attach rates, customer concentration, and gross-margin persistence after automation would materially change the range.
q.rationale: Carrier, government, distributor, and enterprise procurement exposes price and component economics. Reliability, certification, scarce RF expertise, proprietary antennas and algorithms, security, interoperability, support, and urgent delivery preserve a meaningful minority of gains.

caveats: Performance, yield, and revenue gains are not all labor substitution.
High-volume commercial devices and low-volume mission-critical radios have very different implementation thresholds.
rho.change_evidence: Audited deployments reporting engineering cycle, test coverage, calibration, defect escapes, forecast accuracy, support resolution, model overrides, field failures, and labor hours saved would narrow the estimate.
rho.rationale: Digital RF models, firmware, test traces, production measurements, field telemetry, and support records favor implementation. Safety-critical and mission-critical use, spectrum regulation, cybersecurity, export controls, sparse field failures, customer qualification, legacy systems, and rapid standards changes constrain autonomous deployment.

s5.bridge: SBA reports that 47% of family-business owners expecting retirement within five years lack a successor. I treat this as broad succession pressure, then discount it because the source is cross-industry and because wireless equipment businesses may sell products or IP, recapitalize, consolidate strategically, or close without a qualifying firm control transfer.
caveats: The SBA source is not industry-specific and does not measure completed transfers.
Product-line, patent, and asset sales may not transfer an eligible operating firm.
s5.change_evidence: PitchBook, GF Data, Axial, and sector transaction records matched to eligible 334220 firms, plus owner-age and succession interviews, would replace the proxy.
s5.rationale: The large specialist population likely includes founder-led firms facing succession or strategic-sale pressure. Customer qualification, security and export requirements, founder-dependent RF expertise, contract concentration, inventory, and technology transitions can impede transfer.

principal_driver: A large specialist population and rich RF, firmware, test, telemetry, and support data create multiple implementable AI use cases.
principal_weakness: Very low labor intensity and wide product heterogeneity make economic gains and demand durability difficult to generalize across targets.
